topic.name: Songs by George Harrison
topic.description: This cluster focuses on the songs of George Harrison, highlighting their themes and historical context within his solo career.

Document 1: This Guitar ( Can't Keep from Crying ) is a song by English musician George Harrison, released on his 1975 studio album Extra Texture.
Harrison wrote the song as a sequel to his popular Beatles composition While My Guitar Gently Weeps.
Document 2: Ding Dong, Ding Dong is a song by English musician George Harrison, written as a New Year's Eve singalong and released in December 1974.
The song's 'Ring out the old, ring in the new' refrain has invited interpretation as Harrison distancing himself from his past as a member of the Beatles.

topic.name: Naval Warships and History
topic.description: This topic encompasses the design, service history, and specifications of various naval warships from different countries and eras.

Document 1: INS Shakti is a Deepak-class fleet tanker in service with the Indian Navy.
Shakti would significantly add to the Indian Navy's ability to conduct and sustain operations distant from the coast.
Document 2: HMS Royal Oak was a Prince Consort-class armoured frigate built for the Royal Navy in the 1860s.
The ship spent most of her career with the Mediterranean Fleet.
Document 3: Kirov was a Project 26 Kirov-class cruiser of the Soviet Navy that served during the Winter War, World War II and into the Cold War.
She bombarded Finnish positions during the Vyborg – Petrozavodsk Offensive in mid-1944.
Document 4: HMS Hereward was an H-class destroyer built for the Royal Navy in the mid-1930s.
The ship was transferred back to the Mediterranean Fleet later that month, and escorted convoys to Malta.
Document 5: SMS Zieten was the first aviso built for the Imperial German Navy.
Zieten proved to be the first torpedo-armed vessel in a series of avisos that ultimately developed into the first light cruisers.
Document 6: HMS Glorious was the second of the Courageous-class battlecruisers built for the Royal Navy during the First World War.
She was sunk by the German battleships Scharnhorst and Gneisenau in the North Sea.
Document 7: Coatit was a torpedo cruiser of the Italian Regia Marina built in the late 1890s.
She saw action during the Italo-Turkish War in 1911 – 12.

designed to connect local industrial communities together

developed at the confluence of rivers as a trading post

travels through mostly rural land between the cities of Ellsworth and Salina

replaced a ferry and gives communities easier access

Bomar played college football, basketball and baseball for Vanderbilt University.

Parrott was the first MLB player from Oregon.
Document 3: Wilfred Barber was a professional first-class cricketer who played for Yorkshire County Cricket Club.

Document 4: Robert Peter 'Mickey' Micelotta is a former American shortstop in Major League Baseball (MLB).
He played 13 total seasons of professional baseball.
Document 5: Ernest William Groth was an American Major League Baseball right-handed pitcher who played for three seasons.
He played for the Cleveland Indians during the 1947 and 1948 seasons.

topic.name: Biodiversity of Species
topic.description: The documents discuss various species of organisms, including ants, fungi, and sharks, highlighting their taxonomy, habitat, and ecological roles.

Document 1: Ochetellus is a genus of ants first described by Steve Shattuck in 1992.
The colonies are found in rotten wood, in the ground, under rocks or stones and in urban areas.
Document 2: Spongiforma thailandica is a species of fungus in the Boletaceae family, genus Spongiforma.
The species was first described scientifically in 2009 by E. Horak, T. Flegel and D.E. Desjardin.
Document 3: Lactarius rufulus, commonly known as the rufous candy cap, is a species of fungus in the Russulaceae family.
The species, known only from California, Arizona, and Mexico, grows on the ground in leaf litter near oak trees.
Document 4: The milk shark (Rhizoprionodon acutus) is a species of requiem shark, and part of the family Carcharhinidae.
This species is common near beaches and in estuaries, and has been recorded swimming up rivers in Cambodia.
Document 5: Boletopsis nothofagi is a fungus in the family Bankeraceae.
It is unknown when exactly the fungus forms its fruit body, but it has so far been found solely in May.
Document 6: Cyathus helenae is a species of fungus in the genus Cyathus, family Nidulariaceae.
The resemblance that Cyathus helenae bears to a miniature bird's nest with eggs is the source for its common name, bird's nest fungi.
Document 7: The horn shark (Heterodontus francisci) is a species of bullhead shark, in the family Heterodontidae.
Slow-moving, generally solitary predators, horn sharks hunt at night inside small home ranges.

topic.name: Historical Castles in England and Beyond
topic.description: This topic encompasses the history, architecture, and significance of various castles built for defense and administrative purposes in England and other regions.

Document 1: Brough Castle is a ruined castle in the village of Brough, Cumbria, England.
The castle was built by William Rufus around 1092.
Document 2: Berkhamsted Castle is a Norman motte-and-bailey castle in Berkhamsted, Hertfordshire.
The castle was built to obtain control of a key route between London and the Midlands during the Norman conquest.
Document 4: Santa Rosa de la Eminencia castle is a colonial castle built in the seventeenth century by the Spanish monarchy in La Asunción, Venezuela.
Its construction started on 24 March 1677 by order of governor Juan Muñoz de Gadea.
Document 5: Cowes Castle, also known as West Cowes Castle, is a Device Fort in Cowes on the Isle of Wight.
Originally built by Henry VIII in 1539 to protect England against the threat of invasion from France.
Document 6: St Catherine's Castle is a Henrician castle in Cornwall, built by Thomas Treffry between approximately 1538 and 1540.
The D-shaped, stone fortification, equipped with five gun-ports for cannon, overlooked the mouth of the River Fowey.
Document 7: Dunstanburgh Castle is a 14th-century fortification on the coast of Northumberland in northern England.
The castle was built by Earl Thomas of Lancaster between 1313 and 1322.

topic.name: Water Bodies and Conservation
topic.description: The documents discuss various water bodies, including lakes, rivers, and state parks, highlighting their ecological significance and conservation efforts.

Document 1: Lake Neepaulin is a small man-made freshwater lake located in Wantage Township in Sussex County, New Jersey.
The creek, lake, and its watershed are located in the Kittatinny Valley.
Document 2: Cloudland Canyon State Park is a 3,485 acre Georgia state park located near Trenton and Cooper Heights on the western edge of Lookout Mountain.
The park features a variety of campsites, cabins, hiking and recreational activities.
Document 3: West Creek is a tributary of Fishing Creek, in Columbia County and Sullivan County, in Pennsylvania.
West Creek has the highest level of biodiversity of any stream in the upper Fishing Creek watershed.
Document 4: The River Brue originates in the parish of Brewham in Somerset, England, and reaches the sea some 50 kilometres west at Burnham-on-Sea.
The Brue Valley Living Landscape is an ecological conservation project based on the Somerset Levels and Moors.
Document 5: Davidson Seamount is a seamount located off the coast of Central California.
Davidson Seamount is biologically diverse, with 237 species and 27 types of deep-sea coral having been identified.
Document 6: Horton Plains National Park in Ohiya is a protected area in the central highlands of Sri Lanka.
The Horton Plains are the headwaters of three major Sri Lankan rivers, the Mahaweli, Kelani, and Walawe.
Document 7: Toby Creek is a tributary of the Susquehanna River in Luzerne County, Pennsylvania.
The entire drainage basin is designated as a Migratory Fishery.

topic.name: Transportation Infrastructure in Norway
topic.description: The documents discuss various aspects of transportation infrastructure, including railway stations and tram systems in Norway.

Document 7: Hallingskeid Station is a train station on the Bergensbanen line in Norway.
It opened along with the central section of the line on 10 June 1908.

topic.name: Historical Figures and Breeds
topic.description: The documents discuss notable historical figures and horse breeds, highlighting their significance and evolution over time.

Document 1: Aqil Agha was the strongman of northern Palestine in the mid-19th century, during Ottoman rule.
He was known for his protection of local Christian and Jewish communities.
Document 2: The Auxois is a horse breed from eastern France, bred for horse meat, agricultural work and leisure pursuits.
The breed reached its peak in the 1930s, but by the 1960s began to decline with the advent of mechanization.
Document 3: The Canadian horse is a strong, well-muscled breed of horse, usually dark in colour.
It contributed to the development of several horse breeds during the 18th century.
Document 4: George Pickingill was an English farm labourer and folk magician known for his magical means to offer cures.
He attracted visitors seeking his magical help.
Document 5: Charles T. Hinde was an American industrialist and riverboat captain who managed many businesses.
He helped spur the economy of the region in southern California.
Document 6: The Exmoor pony is a horse breed native to the British Isles and is considered endangered.
It contributes to the conservation and management of natural pasture habitats.
Document 7: The Forage War was a partisan campaign during the American Revolutionary War involving skirmishes in New Jersey.
Continental Army regulars and militia companies engaged in scouting and harassing operations against British forces.

topic.name: Culinary Traditions and Innovations
topic.description: The documents explore various culinary traditions, iconic dishes, and the evolution of food products across different cultures and regions.

1: Ya Kun Kaya Toast is a Singaporean chain of mass-market cafes selling toast products, notably kaya toast.
Ya Kun developed a reputation for delicious kaya toast and friendly service.
2: A pasty is a baked pastry particularly associated with Cornwall, made by placing an uncooked filling on one half of a pastry circle.
The traditional Cornish pasty is filled with beef, potato, swede, and onion.
3: Pink slime is a meat-based product used as a food additive to ground beef and processed meats.
The product is regulated in different manners in various regions.
4: Burger King has had a variety of grilled chicken sandwiches in their product portfolio since 1990.
Burger King was the first major fast food chain to introduce a grilled chicken sandwich to the marketplace.
5: Palestinian cuisine consists of foods commonly eaten by Palestinians, influenced by various cultures.
Cooking styles vary by region and are based on the climate and traditions.
6: Mary's Club is the oldest strip club in Portland, known for its entertainment and history.
The club has become a Portland institution, featuring long-term dancers.
7: The cuisine of the Thirteen Colonies includes foods and cooking methods before the American revolution.
The colonial diet was supplemented by new animal and plant foods indigenous to the New World.

topic.name: Poetry and Historical Context
topic.description: The documents explore various poetic works that engage with historical themes and figures, reflecting on the impact of history and personal experiences in shaping poetic expression.

Document 1: 'Last Post' is a poem written by Carol Ann Duffy... commissioned by the BBC to mark the deaths of Henry Allingham and Harry Patch... makes explicit references to Wilfred Owen's poem from the First World War.
The poem imagines what would happen if time ran backwards and those killed in the war came back to life.
Document 2: In 1819, John Keats composed six odes... represent Keats's attempt to create a new type of short lyrical poem, which influenced later generations.
Some critics contend that they form a thematic whole if arranged in sequence.
Document 3: Joan of Arc is a 1796 epic poem composed by Robert Southey... the subject further appealed to Southey because the events of the French Revolution were concurrent to the writing of the poem.
The poem serves as a way for Southey to express his views on history and on politics.
Document 4: Abu Ali Iyad was a senior Palestinian field commander... fought Israeli forces at the Battle of Karameh, gaining a reputation as an unyielding commander.
He was one of the last Palestinian commanders to fight the Jordanian Army in the aftermath of the Black September conflict.
Document 5: Sonnet 18, often alternatively titled Shall I compare thee to a summer's day?, is one of the best-known of 154 sonnets written by William Shakespeare.
The speaker compares his beloved to the summer season, and argues that his beloved is better.
Document 6: Hymns for the Amusement of Children was the final work completed by English poet Christopher Smart... intended to teach Christian virtues.
Smart's Hymns are one of the first works of hymns dedicated to children.
Document 7: Oh Pray My Wings Are Gonna Fit Me Well is a book of poems by American author Maya Angelou... the volume is dedicated to 'Paul'.
The poetry in the volume has been characterized as light verse, focusing on themes of love, insight, and tension.

topic.name: Human Spaceflight Programs
topic.description: The documents discuss various historical human spaceflight programs and missions conducted by NASA and other organizations, highlighting key events, figures, and technological advancements.

Document 1: Apollo program was the third United States human spaceflight program carried out by NASA, accomplishing landing the first humans on the Moon from 1969 to 1972.
Apollo ran from 1961 to 1972, with the first manned flight in 1968.
Document 3: Soyuz TM-30 was a Soyuz mission, the 39th and final human spaceflight to the Mir space station.
The mission was the first privately funded mission to a space station.
Document 4: Apollo 1 was the first manned mission of the United States Apollo program, which had as its ultimate goal a manned lunar landing.
A cabin fire during a launch rehearsal test on January 27 killed all three crew members.
Document 5: Advanced Gemini is a number of proposals that would have extended the Gemini program by the addition of various missions, including manned low Earth orbit, circumlunar and lunar landing missions.
Gemini was the second manned spaceflight program operated by NASA.

topic.name: Bach's Church Cantatas

Document 1: BWV 86 is a church cantata by Johann Sebastian Bach.

Document 2: BWV 23 is a church cantata by Johann Sebastian Bach.

Document 5: BWV 40 is a church cantata by Johann Sebastian Bach.

topic.name: Cultural and Historical Monuments
topic.description: The documents discuss various cultural and historical monuments that reflect the artistic, educational, and social heritage of different regions.

Document 1: Agustín Ross Cultural Centre, previously known as Casino Ross, is the cultural center of the city of Pichilemu in Chile.
The former casino was declared a National Monument of Chile along with the Agustín Ross Park in February 1988.
Document 2: Helen C. White was a professor of English at the University of Wisconsin – Madison and the first woman elected president of the American Association of University Professors.
She wrote six novels and numerous nonfiction books and articles.
Document 3: Pod is a modern sculpture by American artist Pete Beeman, intended to represent the infrastructure, energy, and vibrancy of Portland.
It is considered interactive and kinetic, with a central, vertical pendulum that swings back and forth when pushed.
Document 4: The Aboriginal Memorial is a work of contemporary Indigenous Australian art comprising 200 decorated hollow log coffins.
It was created to coincide with the Australian Bicentenary and commemorates those Indigenous Australians who died as a result of European settlement.
Document 6: The Taras Shevchenko Memorial is a bronze statue honoring the Ukrainian poet and artist Taras Shevchenko.
It was dedicated in 1964, the 150th anniversary of Shevchenko's birth.
Document 7: The Croatian Natural History Museum is the oldest and biggest natural history museum in Croatia, with over 2 million artefacts.
It was founded in 1846 as the 'National Museum' and contains a large scientific library open to the public.

topic.name: Scientific Principles and Concepts
topic.description: The documents discuss various fundamental scientific principles and concepts across different fields such as physics, chemistry, mathematics, and engineering.

1: Inverted-F antenna is a type of antenna used in wireless communication.
The design has two advantages over a simple monopole: the antenna is shorter and more compact.
2: Newton's theorem of revolving orbits identifies the type of central force needed to multiply the angular speed of a particle.
Isaac Newton derived this theorem in Propositions 43 – 45 of Book I of his Philosophiæ Naturalis Principia Mathematica.
3: The Avogadro constant is the number of constituent particles contained in the amount of substance given by one mole.
It has the value 6.022140857 × 10^23 mol − 1 in the International System of Units.
4: Fleiss' kappa is a statistical measure for assessing the reliability of agreement between a fixed number of raters.
It calculates the degree of agreement in classification over that which would be expected by chance.
5: The number e is an important mathematical constant that is the base of the natural logarithm.
It is approximately equal to 2.71828 and is the limit of (1 + 1/n)^n as n approaches infinity.
6: Prototype filters are electronic filter designs that are used as a template to produce a modified filter design.
They are an example of a nondimensionalised design from which the desired filter can be scaled or transformed.
7: The Mayer – Vietoris sequence is an algebraic tool to help compute algebraic invariants of topological spaces.
The method consists of splitting a space into subspaces, for which the homology or cohomology groups may be easier to compute.

topic.name: Legal and Political History
topic.description: The documents explore significant legal and political figures, institutions, and events that have shaped governance and civil liberties in various countries.

Document 1: The Court of Common Pleas was a common law court in the English legal system that covered 'common pleas'; actions between subject and subject, which did not concern the king.
The Common Pleas maintained its exclusive jurisdiction over matters of real property until its dissolution.
Document 2: Jon Gundersen Hol was a Norwegian engineer and activist known for his pamphlet Rifleringen, which called for armed civilian defense of the Parliament of Norway.
The political situation in Norway at the time was unstable, with an ongoing impeachment case against the conservative government.
Document 3: Raúl Ricardo Alfonsín served as the President of Argentina from 10 December 1983, to 8 July 1989.
He opposed both sides of the Dirty War and filed a writ of Habeas corpus for victims of forced disappearances.
Document 4: Article 14 of the Constitution of the Republic of Singapore guarantees to Singapore citizens the rights to freedom of speech and expression, peaceful assembly without arms, and association.
The enjoyment of these rights may be restricted by laws imposed by the Parliament of Singapore on the grounds stated in Article 14 (2).
Document 5: Mildred Lewis Rutherford was a prominent educator and author from Athens, Georgia, heavily involved in many organizations, including the United Daughters of the Confederacy.
She held strong pro-Confederacy, proslavery views and opposed women's suffrage.
Document 6: Ronald Marc George is the retired 27th Chief Justice of California, where he headed the Supreme Court of California.
As a Deputy Attorney General, George argued cases before the United States Supreme Court.
Document 7: The American Civil Liberties Union (ACLU) is a nonpartisan organization whose mission is to defend and preserve individual rights and liberties guaranteed by the Constitution.
The ACLU provides legal assistance in cases when it considers civil liberties to be at risk.

Document 4: The NSB El 17 is a class of twelve electric locomotives built by Thyssen-Henschel and Norsk Elektrisk & Brown Boveri (NEBB) for the Norwegian State Railways (NSB).
The traction system of the El 17 was based on the DB Class 120 of Germany.
